CharCoat IC
Insulator Coa(cid:415)ng
PRODUCT DESCRIPTION
CharCoat IC is a unique, room temperature vulcanising
(RTV) silicone rubber formula(cid:415)on, specifically developed by
CharCoat Passive Fire Protec(cid:415)on, Mace Technologies and in
collabora(cid:415)on with the Electric Power Research Group of the
University of the Witwatersrand (South Africa), for the preven(cid:415)on
of pollu(cid:415)on flashover of high voltage outdoor insula(cid:415)on. ADVANTAGES
The superior performance of hydrophobic silicone over other
APPLICATION AREAS insula(cid:415)ng materials in the most contaminated environments is well
CharCoat IC should be applied wherever pollu(cid:415)on related proven. What makes the silicone unique is the fact that its water
flashovers of high voltage equipment insula(cid:415)on pose a significant repellency increases with (cid:415)me and that, owing to the migra(cid:415)on of
threat to supply reliability. The coa(cid:415)ng of an insulator with this low molecular weight silanes that exist within the elastomer, these
water repellent silicone elastomer inhibits the forma(cid:415)on of water repellent proper(cid:415)es are imparted to contaminants which
conduc(cid:415)ng films on the insulator surface. This, in turn, ensures that land on the surface. Leakage current ac(cid:415)vity is thus kept to a
dry band discharges – the cri(cid:415)cal stage in pollu(cid:415)on flashover minimum and surface erosion is prevented.The CharCoat IC curing
process – will not occur. Even in heavy pollu(cid:415)on, an insulator coated system has the benefits of not requiring any atmospheric moisture
with CharCoat IC will retain a flashover voltage significantly higher for vulcanisa(cid:415)on to take place, the cure rate is independent of
than the opera(cid:415)onal stresses. humidity, no by-products are released on curing and there is
prac(cid:415)cally no shrinkage.
TECHNICAL DATA FEATURES AND BENEFITS
• Excellent arc resistance
PROPERTIES RESULT
• Fast hydrophobicity transfer to contamina(cid:415)ng layers
Specific gravity @23°C 1.01 g/cm(cid:1057) • Fast recovery of hydrophobicity a(cid:332)er arcing
Viscosity 33.8 cSt • Resistance to damage by corona discharge
• Adhesion to glass, ceramic and resin surfaces
Color Grey / Sky Blue
• Resistant to oil, ozone, various chemicals and petrols
Dielectric strength @23°C 64 kV/mm
• Ageing resistant
Surface resis(cid:415)vity 1.5 x 1013 Ω ohm
• 100% UV resistant
Volume resis(cid:415)vity 2.29 x 1013 Ωm ohm metre • A(cid:332)er comple(cid:415)on (full cure) temperature resistant down to -50°C
Hydrophobicity 122(cid:481) contact angle
DIRECTION FOR USE:
Arc res(cid:415)stance > 185 seconds
Recommended condi(cid:415)ons
Tracking & erosion > 470 minutes @ 2.5kV
1. Material and air temperature > 5°C, humidity < 80%
Hydrophobicity recovery A(cid:332)er 100 hours corona test: HC6
2. Recommended (cid:415)p size for airless spraying: 0.010" – 0.012"
A(cid:332)er 48 hours: HC1-HC2 3. Pressure at least 6-7 bar
Skin over (cid:415)me @23°C 20 minutes 4. Fan width approx 15cm
5. Refer to complete applica(cid:415)on guide
PREPARATION BEFORE APPLICATION
IMPORTANT:CharCoatCChastobes(cid:415)rredthoroughlybefore
applica(cid:415)on
• All insulators to be clean and free of dust/oils/solids
• All surfaces must be primed with CharCoat IC Primer
• Catalyst must be added
• Mixing by a slow running s(cid:415)rrer
• Protect all areas not being coated from overspray
• Applica(cid:415)on by airless spraying or by compressor, high
pressure hose and spray gun

rows: CharCoat IC Insulator Coa(cid:415)ng PRODUCT DESCRIPTION CharCoat IC is a unique, room temperature vulcanising (RTV) silicone rubber formula(cid:415)on, specifically developed by CharCoat Passive Fire Protec(cid:415)on, Mace Technologies and in collabora(cid:415)on with the Electric Power Research Group of the University of the Witwatersrand (South Africa), for the preven(cid:415)on of pollu(cid:415)on flashover of high voltage outdoor insula(cid:415)on. ADVANTAGES The superior performance of hydrophobic silicone over other APPLICATION AREAS insula(cid:415)ng materials in the most contaminated environments is well CharCoat IC should be applied wherever pollu(cid:415)on related proven. What makes the silicone unique is the fact that its water flashovers of high voltage equipment insula(cid:415)on pose a significant repellency increases with (cid:415)me and that, owing to the migra(cid:415)on of threat to supply reliability. The coa(cid:415)ng of an insulator with this low molecular weight silanes that exist within the elastomer, these water repellent silicone elastomer inhibits the forma(cid:415)on of water repellent proper(cid:415)es are imparted to contaminants which conduc(cid:415)ng films on the insulator surface. This, in turn, ensures that land on the surface. Leakage current ac(cid:415)vity is thus kept to a dry band discharges – the cri(cid:415)cal stage in pollu(cid:415)on flashover minimum and surface erosion is prevented.The CharCoat IC curing process – will not occur. Even in heavy pollu(cid:415)on, an insulator coated system has the benefits of not requiring any atmospheric moisture with CharCoat IC will retain a flashover voltage significantly higher for vulcanisa(cid:415)on to take place, the cure rate is independent of than the opera(cid:415)onal stresses. humidity, no by-products are released on curing and there is prac(cid:415)cally no shrinkage. Material and air temperature > 5°C, humidity < 80% Hydrophobicity recovery A(cid:332)er 100 hours corona test: HC6 2. Recommended (cid:415)p size for airless spraying: 0.010" – 0.012" A(cid:332)er 48 hours: HC1-HC2 3. Pressure at least 6-7 bar Skin over (cid:415)me @23°C 20 minutes 4. Fan width approx 15cm 5. Refer to complete applica(cid:415)on guide PREPARATION BEFORE APPLICATION IMPORTANT:CharCoatCChastobes(cid:415)rredthoroughlybefore applica(cid:415)on • All insulators to be clean and free of dust/oils/solids • All surfaces must be primed with CharCoat IC Primer • Catalyst must be added • Mixing by a slow running s(cid:415)rrer • Protect all areas not being coated from overspray • Applica(cid:415)on by airless spraying or by compressor, high pressure hose and spray gun

| CharCoat IC Insulator Coa(cid:415)ng PRODUCT DESCRIPTION CharCoat IC is a unique, room temperature vulcanising (RTV) silicone rubber formula(cid:415)on, specifically developed by CharCoat Passive Fire Protec(cid:415)on, Mace Technologies and in collabora(cid:415)on with the Electric Power Research Group of the University of the Witwatersrand (South Africa), for the preven(cid:415)on of pollu(cid:415)on flashover of high voltage outdoor insula(cid:415)on. ADVANTAGES The superior performance of hydrophobic silicone over other APPLICATION AREAS insula(cid:415)ng materials in the most contaminated environments is well CharCoat IC should be applied wherever pollu(cid:415)on related proven. What makes the silicone unique is the fact that its water flashovers of high voltage equipment insula(cid:415)on pose a significant repellency increases with (cid:415)me and that, owing to the migra(cid:415)on of threat to supply reliability. The coa(cid:415)ng of an insulator with this low molecular weight silanes that exist within the elastomer, these water repellent silicone elastomer inhibits the forma(cid:415)on of water repellent proper(cid:415)es are imparted to contaminants which conduc(cid:415)ng films on the insulator surface. This, in turn, ensures that land on the surface. Leakage current ac(cid:415)vity is thus kept to a dry band discharges – the cri(cid:415)cal stage in pollu(cid:415)on flashover minimum and surface erosion is prevented.The CharCoat IC curing process – will not occur. Even in heavy pollu(cid:415)on, an insulator coated system has the benefits of not requiring any atmospheric moisture with CharCoat IC will retain a flashover voltage significantly higher for vulcanisa(cid:415)on to take place, the cure rate is independent of than the opera(cid:415)onal stresses. humidity, no by-products are released on curing and there is prac(cid:415)cally no shrinkage. TECHNICAL DATA FEATURES AND BENEFITS • Excellent arc resistance PROPERTIES RESULT • Fast hydrophobicity transfer to contamina(cid:415)ng layers Specific gravity @23°C 1.01 g/cm(cid:1057) • Fast recovery of hydrophobicity a(cid:332)er arcing Viscosity 33.8 cSt • Resistance to damage by corona discharge • Adhesion to glass, ceramic and resin surfaces Color Grey / Sky Blue • Resistant to oil, ozone, various chemicals and petrols Dielectric strength @23°C 64 kV/mm • Ageing resistant Surface resis(cid:415)vity 1.5 x 1013 Ω ohm • 100% UV resistant Volume resis(cid:415)vity 2.29 x 1013 Ωm ohm metre • A(cid:332)er comple(cid:415)on (full cure) temperature resistant down to -50°C Hydrophobicity 122(cid:481) contact angle DIRECTION FOR USE: Arc res(cid:415)stance > 185 seconds Recommended condi(cid:415)ons Tracking & erosion > 470 minutes @ 2.5kV 1. Material and air temperature > 5°C, humidity < 80% Hydrophobicity recovery A(cid:332)er 100 hours corona test: HC6 2. Recommended (cid:415)p size for airless spraying: 0.010" – 0.012" A(cid:332)er 48 hours: HC1-HC2 3. Pressure at least 6-7 bar Skin over (cid:415)me @23°C 20 minutes 4. Fan width approx 15cm 5. Refer to complete applica(cid:415)on guide PREPARATION BEFORE APPLICATION IMPORTANT:CharCoatCChastobes(cid:415)rredthoroughlybefore applica(cid:415)on • All insulators to be clean and free of dust/oils/solids • All surfaces must be primed with CharCoat IC Primer • Catalyst must be added • Mixing by a slow running s(cid:415)rrer • Protect all areas not being coated from overspray • Applica(cid:415)on by airless spraying or by compressor, high pressure hose and spray gun |

images.context_text: CharCoat IC Insulator Coa(cid:415)ng PRODUCT DESCRIPTION CharCoat IC is a unique, room temperature vulcanising (RTV) silicone rubber formula(cid:415)on, specifically developed by CharCoat Passive Fire Protec(cid:415)on, Mace Technologies and in collabora(cid:415)on with the Electric Power Research Group of the University of the Witwatersrand (South Africa), for the preven(cid:415)on of pollu(cid:415)on flashover of high voltage outdoor insula(cid:415)on. ADVANTAGES The superior performance of hydrophobic silicone over other APPLICATION AREAS insula(cid:415)ng materials in the most contaminated environments is well CharCoat IC should be applied wherever pollu(cid:415)on related proven. What makes the silicone unique is the fact that its water flashovers of high voltage equipment insula(cid:415)on pose a significant repellency increases with (cid:415)me and that, owing to the migra(cid:415)on of threat to supply reliability. The coa(cid:415)ng of an insulator with this low molecular weight silanes that exist within the elastomer, these water repellent silicone elastomer inhibits the forma(cid:415)on of water repellent proper(cid:415)es are imparted to contaminants which conduc(cid:415)ng films on the insulator surface. This, in turn, ensures that land on the surface. Leakage current ac(cid:415)vity is thus kept to a dry band discharges – the cri(cid:415)cal stage in pollu(cid:415)on flashover minimum and surface erosion is prevented.The CharCoat IC curing process – will not occur. Even in heavy pollu(cid:415)on, an insulator coated system has the benefits of not requiring any atmospheric moisture with CharCoat IC will retain a flashover voltage significantly higher for vulcanisa(cid:415)on to take place, the cure rate is independent of than the opera(cid:415)onal stresses. humidity, no by-products are released on curing and there is prac(cid:415)cally no shrinkage. TECHNICAL DATA FEATURES AND BENEFITS • Excellent arc resistance PROPERTIES RESULT • Fast hydrophobicity transfer to contamina(cid:415)ng layers Specific gravity @23°C 1.01 g/cm(cid:1057) • Fast recovery of hydrophobicity a(cid:332)er arcing Viscosity 33.8 cSt • Resistance to damage by corona discharge • Adhesion to glass, ceramic and resin surfaces Color Grey / Sky Blue • Resistant to oil, ozone, various chemicals and petrols Dielectric strength @23°C 64 kV/mm • Ageing resistant Surface resis(cid:415)vity 1.5 x 1013 Ω ohm • 100% UV resistant Volume resis(cid:415)vity 2.29 x 1013 Ωm ohm metre •
